skill.name: similarity-search-patterns
skill.description: Build similarity search and vector retrieval patterns for Pinecone, Qdrant, pgvector, and Weaviate implementations.
skill.summary: Create production-ready vector search patterns for semantic search, RAG retrieval, hybrid search, and vector database tuning.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, credential, weak crypto, reconnaissance, and combined-danger patterns. Most are false positives from Markdown code fences, database client examples, API-key parameters, and documentation links; however, the pgvector template interpolates metadata keys into SQL and should be published with a security warning.

medium_findings.title: SQL Identifier Injection Risk in pgvector Filter
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE semantic issue. The pgvector search example parameterizes filter values but interpolates metadata keys directly into a SQL condition. If copied into an application that accepts user-controlled filter keys, this can permit SQL injection or query manipulation. No malicious intent or exfiltration behavior was found.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The SQL value is parameterized, but the metadata key is inserted with string formatting before query execution. This is a clear copy-paste vulnerability when filter keys can come from untrusted input.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Are Markdown Fence False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown code fence delimiters around documentation examples, not executable code paths. No shell execution API or command invocation was found in the inspected skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens are the opening and closing triple-backtick fences in Markdown. The surrounding content is explanatory Python sample code, not a runnable installer or script.

low_findings.title: Network Findings Are Client Examples and Documentation Links
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for malicious networking. The conn.fetch calls are PostgreSQL database reads, the Weaviate URL defaults to localhost, and the hardcoded URLs are public documentation links. These examples may contact configured vector services if used by a developer, but they do not exfiltrate data from the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network-related lines are either database client method calls or reference links. No credential-bearing request to an unexpected external endpoint was found.

low_findings.title: Credential Access Findings Are API-Key Parameters
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for secret harvesting. The Pinecone example accepts an api_key parameter and passes it to the official client constructor. The skill does not read environment variables, enumerate secrets, log keys, or send keys to an unrelated endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The key is a constructor argument in sample code and is only passed to Pinecone(api_key=api_key). There is no process environment access or unauthorized transmission pattern.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Findings Are Scanner Noise
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The weak-crypto and system-reconnaissance detections do not correspond to cryptographic operations, host enumeration, or sensitive system discovery in the inspected context. The relevant lines are metadata, SQL setup, joins, or general best-practice text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no md5, sha1, host probing, environment discovery, or similar sensitive behavior at these locations. The static labels appear triggered by unrelated words and SQL syntax.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for malicious intent. The static tool combined code-fence, network, and API-key parameter detections into a critical heuristic, but manual review found a documentation-only skill with no executable installer, no prompt injection attempt, and no data exfiltration flow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains only Markdown guidance and examples. The heuristic ingredients are explainable as documentation constructs, though the SQL filter issue remains a real medium-risk example vulnerability.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unescaped SQL Identifier Construction
dangerous_patterns.description: The pgvector template builds a SQL condition with an interpolated metadata key. Implementations should whitelist metadata keys or quote identifiers safely before using this pattern.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly visible in the SQL construction block and can become exploitable with untrusted filter keys.

content.value_statement: Teams need reliable retrieval patterns before scaling semantic search or RAG systems. This skill gives concrete templates for vector stores, hybrid search, reranking, and index tuning.
actual_capabilities: Explains distance metrics and index choices for vector retrieval systems.
Provides Pinecone, Qdrant, pgvector, and Weaviate implementation templates.
Shows vector search, filtered search, hybrid search, and reranking patterns.
Includes batching, index creation, score handling, and metadata return examples.
Summarizes tuning practices for recall, latency, storage, and query quality.
limitations: Examples require adaptation before production use, especially SQL filter handling.
It does not benchmark your data, embeddings, or vector database deployment.
It does not manage secrets, credentials, migrations, or infrastructure provisioning.
Library APIs can change, so generated code should be checked against current vendor docs.

use_cases.title: Launch Semantic Search
use_cases.description: Use the templates to add vector search with metadata and score handling to an application.

use_cases.title: Improve RAG Retrieval
use_cases.description: Combine vector search, keyword search, and reranking to improve retrieved context quality.

use_cases.title: Choose a Vector Backend
use_cases.description: Compare Pinecone, Qdrant, pgvector, and Weaviate patterns before selecting an implementation path.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Search Store
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new semantic search feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a simple vector search store for my documents. Include the database choice, schema, upsert flow, and search method.

prompt_templates.title: Add Metadata Filters
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate filtering for tenant, source, or document type.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add safe metadata filtering to my vector search. Show how to validate filter keys and keep values parameterized.

prompt_templates.title: Design Hybrid Retrieval
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced retrieval that combines semantic and keyword matching.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design hybrid retrieval for RAG. Include vector scoring, keyword scoring, weighting, reranking, and evaluation metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Production Search
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced scaling for latency, recall, and cost targets.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my similarity search architecture for production scale. Recommend index type, tuning parameters, batching, monitoring, and failure handling.

output_examples.input: I need semantic search for product documentation with source filters.
output_examples.output: A design using a vector database collection, document metadata fields, safe filter validation, batched upserts, and top-k search results with scores.

output_examples.input: My RAG answers miss exact product names and version numbers.
output_examples.output: A hybrid retrieval plan that combines vector similarity with keyword matching, then reranks a larger candidate set before context assembly.

output_examples.input: Our vector search is slow at one million documents.
output_examples.output: A tuning checklist covering HNSW or quantized indexes, recall testing, batch sizes, filtering strategy, and latency monitoring.

best_practices: Validate filter fields against a strict allowlist before building database queries.
Measure recall and latency with representative queries before changing index settings.
Start with clear metadata, batching, and observability before adding complex reranking.
anti_patterns: Do not copy SQL string interpolation patterns without identifier validation.
Do not assume one vector database or index type is best for every dataset.
Do not tune for average latency while ignoring recall and tail latency.

faq.question: Which vector databases does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers Pinecone, Qdrant, pgvector, and Weaviate through implementation templates and retrieval patterns.

faq.question: Can I use it for RAG systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes patterns for semantic retrieval, hybrid search, reranking, and result scoring for RAG workflows.

faq.question: Does it provide production-ready code?
faq.answer: It provides strong starting templates, but you must adapt security, validation, credentials, and operations for your environment.

faq.question: What is the main security concern?
faq.answer: The pgvector filter example needs safe metadata key validation before use with untrusted input.

faq.question: Does it execute commands or install packages?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown guide with examples and does not include an executable installer or script.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
